meta.title: PhD's Veterinary Clinic | Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay
meta.description: Surgical, orthopedic, and internal medicine services under the management of Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay at Antalya Liman. Expert veterinary care for your pets.

general.whatsappMessage: Hello, I would like to make an appointment.

hero.title:
The Best for
Our Friends
hero.subtitle: PhD's Veterinary Clinic, managed by Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay, is a clinic that offers modern and reliable veterinary services. We are here to provide the best care for the health and happiness of your pets.

services.title: What Do We Offer?
services.subtitle: The services shown here are just the beginning. Every pet has different needs, so please feel free to contact us for your special requests.

items.title: Trauma and fracture management
items.desc: It is crucial for cats and dogs injured by trauma to regain their pre-trauma functional state. Poor management of the process leads to bone and soft tissue infections (osteomyelitis). Healing time prolongs and may result in unwanted outcomes like limb loss.

items.title: Spaying and Neutering Services
items.desc: Neutering is a major surgical operation. Complications include Ovarian Remnant Syndrome in females causing continued heat and infection. in males, undescended testicles (cryptorchidism) in the inguinal canal or abdomen make the operation more complex.

items.title: Preventive medicine and consultancy
items.desc: Vaccines and effective antiparasitic applications protect your cats and dogs from many diseases. Also, zoonotic diseases transmitted from animals to humans are prevented by these applications.

items.title: Professionalism in Surgical Operations
items.desc: Transparent treatment process with minimum complications, fast and functional recovery in surgical operations.

items.title: Radiology and Ultrasonography Services
items.desc: Advanced radiological and ultrasonography services for comprehensive diagnosis.

items.title: Internal Medicine Services
items.desc: Diagnosis and treatment of internal diseases in cats and dogs.

items.title: Lameness management
items.desc: Diagnosis and treatment of genetic and developmental diseases causing lameness such as patellar luxation, cruciate ligament ruptures, hip dysplasia, and joint calcification.

items.title: Neurosurgery
items.desc: Diagnosis and treatment of complicated procedures such as cervical, thoracic and lumbar disc herniations (IVDD), nervous system tumor surgery, and spinal fractures.

items.title: Mouth, nose and ear surgery
items.desc: Jaw fractures, oral tumor surgery, upper respiratory tract surgeries in brachycephalic breeds (Rhinoplasty, Frontal sinus, etc.), bulla osteotomy, and total ear canal ablation (TECA).

items.title: Post-traumatic rehabilitation
items.desc: Physical therapy, hydrotherapy, and other rehabilitation services during the post-trauma recovery process.

reviews.title: What Our Customers Say?
reviews.subtitle: Experiences of our valued customers who chose us.

items.name: Betül Bayraktar
items.comment: Thanks to our cat Fındık, our paths crossed with Soner Bey. He is a smiling doctor and a very successful surgeon in his work. We thank you for your interest and attention. It is a Veterinary Clinic that you can go to and trust with your eyes closed. I recommend it to everyone ☺️

items.name: Shoaib Husnain
items.comment: Dr Soner is the best orthopedic and neuro surgeon.

items.name: Abidin Yılmaz
items.comment: Some professions require knowledge, some require conscience. Soner Çağatay has both in abundance.

about.title: About Our Doctor
about.name: Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay
about.bio: I graduated from Ankara University Faculty of Veterinary Medicine in 2003. I started my specialization training in the Department of Surgery at the same university in 2006. In 2007, I was appointed as a staff assistant in the Department of Traumatology and Orthopedic Surgery. As Dr. Soner Çağatay, I received the title of Science Specialist in 2009 and Doctor of Veterinary Surgery (PhD) in 2015. During this time, I personally performed surgical operations and gained high experience in surgical operation knowledge. Also during this period, I participated in clinical and scientific studies in Germany, the Netherlands, and many other countries. I attended the Medical Training course at Berlin Freie University and increased my knowledge and skills in small animal surgery. Post-doctorate, I carried out post-doctoral projects and studies at Ankara University. In 2017, I transferred to Near East University in the TRNC and shared my knowledge and skills with Cypriot veterinarian candidates. I actively managed surgical operations and taught the undergraduate Traumatology and Orthopedic Surgery course in both English and Turkish. As Veterinary Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay, I continue to serve at PhD’s Veterinary Clinic in Antalya Liman Neighborhood with over 20 years of professional experience.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.subtitle: Feel free to contact us for your questions.

publications.title: Academic Research & Articles
publications.subtitle: International scientific publications by Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay in the field of small animal surgery, orthopedics, and traumatology.

items.title: Clinical and Radiographical Assessments of Treatment of Salter-Harris Type Fractures in Cat and Dog
items.originalTitle: Clinical and radiographical assessments of treatment of Salter-Harris type fractures in cat and dog
items.journal: Journal of Ankara University Faculty of Veterinary Medicine
items.desc: A study investigating the clinical and radiological success of surgical and conservative treatments of growth plate (Salter-Harris) fractures in cats and dogs.

items.title: Clinical and Radiological Evaluation of Triple Tibial Osteotomy Technique for Treatment of Ligamentum Cruciatum Anterior Rupture in Dogs
items.originalTitle: Clinical and radiological evaluation of triple tibial osteotomy technique for treatment of ligamentum cruciatum anterior rupture in dogs
items.journal: Journal of the Turkish Veterinary Medical Association
items.desc: Clinical follow-up and assessment of the modern Triple Tibial Osteotomy (TTO) technique applied in cranial cruciate ligament ruptures in dogs.

items.title: Dorsal Scapular Luxation and its Surgical Treatment in a Cat
items.originalTitle: Dorsal scapular luxation and its surgical treatment in a cat
items.journal: Journal of Turkish Veterinary Medical Society
items.desc: A case report involving the orthopedic wiring and surgical stabilization of traumatic dorsal scapular luxation in a cat, resulting in successful recovery.

items.title: Removal of a Tracheal Foreign Body by Intercostal Thoracotomy in a Cat
items.originalTitle: Removal of a tracheal foreign body by intercostal thoracotomy in a cat
items.desc: A case report describing the successful extraction of a foreign body from a cat's trachea using advanced thoracic surgery (intercostal thoracotomy).

items.title: Physiotherapy in Dogs with Hip Osteoarthritis – Effects On Pain, Muscle and Lameness
items.originalTitle: Physiotherapy in Dogs with Hip Osteoarthritis – Effects On Pain, Muscle and Lameness
items.journal: Turkish Journal of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation
items.desc: A randomized controlled trial measuring the clinical efficacy of physical therapy, hydrotherapy, and exercise programs in managing pain and mobility in dogs with hip arthritis.

items.title: Hypertrophic Osteopathy Associated With Intrathoracic Masses in 5 Dogs and Review of the Literature
items.originalTitle: Hypertrophic Osteopathy Associated With Intrathoracic Masses in 5 Dogs and Review of the Literature
items.desc: Diagnosis and clinical follow-up of Marie-Bamberger syndrome (painful bone thickening in limbs) secondary to lung tumors and chest masses in dogs.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions pet owners ask about surgeries, treatments, and our clinic.

items.question: Do you offer emergency surgery services in your clinic?
items.answer: Yes, emergency surgeries for trauma, road accidents, falls, and fractures are performed by our surgical team led by Specialist Dr. Soner Çağatay.

items.question: What should be considered after spaying or neutering surgery in cats and dogs?
items.answer: A quiet and warm resting area should be provided for the first 24 hours. Prescribed pain relief and protective medication must be given on time, and a collar (E-collar) must be worn to prevent licking or damaging the sutures.

items.question: Can patellar luxation (kneecap dislocation) be treated without surgery?
items.answer: In mild cases (Grade 1 and sometimes Grade 2), the clinical condition can be stabilized with weight management, muscle-strengthening physiotherapy, hydrotherapy, and joint supplements. However, for advanced dislocation and lameness, surgery is the definitive solution.

items.question: Which surgical methods are used for cranial cruciate ligament (ACL) rupture in dogs?
items.answer: Depending on the dog's weight, age, and knee biomechanics, TPLO, TTO (Triple Tibial Osteotomy), or lateral suture techniques are performed. Dr. Soner Çağatay has extensive experience in the TTO technique which provides excellent biomechanical recovery.

items.question: Do you provide post-operative physiotherapy services at your clinic?
items.answer: Yes, we offer customized physiotherapy and rehabilitation programs after orthopedic and neurological surgeries to prevent joint stiffness, maintain muscle mass, and ensure fast functional recovery.
